قل يتوفاكم ملك الموت الذي وكل بكم ثم إلى ربكم ترجعون السجدة 11
ونادوا يا مالك ليقض علينا ربك قال إنكم ماكثون الزخرف 77
بأيدي سفرة عبس 15 كرام بررة عبس 16
"مثل الذي يقرأ القرآن وهو حافظ له مع السفرة الكرام" البخاري
"ألا استحيي من رجل تستحي منه الملائكة" مسلم عائشة كشف عن فخذيه أو ساقيه

queries: الزخرف 77 ونادوا يا مالك ليقض علينا ربك قال إنكم ماكثون
البقرة 98 من كان عدوا لله وملائكته ورسله وجبريل وميكال فإن الله عدو للكافرين
"مَنْ كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِلَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ" البقرة 98 quran

output.code:
# Load the user's text exactly as stored, then locate all quoted citations and compute precise spans.
# We'll parse:
# - Double-quoted Quran citations " ... "
# - Double-parenthesis hadith citations (( ... ))
# For the Abasa quote that spans two ayat, we'll also compute subspans for each ayah: "بِأَيْدِي سَفَرَةٍ" and "كِرَامٍ بَرَرَةٍ".

from pathlib import Path
import re, json, textwrap, sys

path = Path("/mnt/data/file-GJmgmYhyDdqFTPqnUymotX-Q121.txt")
text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

# We'll only consider the content before the "References:" section for citation extraction.
ref_idx = text.find("References:")
main_text = text if ref_idx == -1 else text[:ref_idx]

# Helper to find all double-quoted segments
quoted_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"\n]+)"', main_text):
    # exclude the surrounding quotes by using group(1)
    inner = m.group(1)
    # calculate absolute positions in the full text (excluding quotes)
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    quoted_spans.append({"type_guess": "Aya", "span_text": inner, "span_start": start, "span_end": end})

# Helper to find all double-parentheses segments
paren_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'[\(（]{2}([\s\S]*?)[\)）]{2}', main_text):
    inner = m.group(1)
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    paren_spans.append({"type_guess": "Hadeeth", "span_text": inner, "span_start": start, "span_end": end})

# Inspect what we found
len(quoted_spans), len(paren_spans), quoted_spans, paren_spans[:1], main_text[:2000]

# Manually locate the Muslim hadith phrase within the text and compute its span
phrase = "ألا استحيي من رجل تستحيي منه الملائكة"
idx = main_text.find(phrase)
idx, phrase, main_text[idx-30:idx+len(phrase)+30]

# Compute subspans for Abasa 15-16 inside the combined quote span
abasa_combined_start = [q for q in quoted_spans if "سَفَرَةٍ كِرَامٍ" in q["span_text"]][0]["span_start"]
abasa_combined_text = [q for q in quoted_spans if "سَفَرَةٍ كِرَامٍ" in q["span_text"]][0]["span_text"]

part1 = "بِأَيْدِي سَفَرَةٍ"
part2 = "كِرَامٍ بَرَرَةٍ"

# Verify the combined text structure
abasa_combined_text, part1 in abasa_combined_text, part2 in abasa_combined_text

# Calculate absolute positions
inner_idx1 = abasa_combined_text.find(part1)
inner_idx2 = abasa_combined_text.find(part2)

start1 = abasa_combined_start + inner_idx1
end1 = start1 + len(part1)

start2 = abasa_combined_start + inner_idx2
end2 = start2 + len(part2)

(start1, end1, abasa_combined_text[inner_idx1:inner_idx1+len(part1)],
 start2, end2, abasa_combined_text[inner_idx2:inner_idx2+len(part2)])

# Gather all the spans we need and prepare a data structure
results = []

# Quran citations:
# 1) Al-Baqarah 2:98
q1 = next(q for q in quoted_spans if q["span_text"].startswith("مَنْ كَانَ عَدُوًّا لِلَّهِ"))
results.append({
    "span_start": q1["span_start"],
    "span_end": q1["span_end"],
    "span_text": q1["span_text"],
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "البقرة", "sura_number": 2, "aya_number": 98},
})

# 2) As-Sajda 32:11
q2 = next(q for q in quoted_spans if q["span_text"].startswith("قُلْ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ مَلَكُ الْمَوْتِ"))
results.append({
    "span_start": q2["span_start"],
    "span_end": q2["span_end"],
    "span_text": q2["span_text"],
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "السجدة", "sura_number": 32, "aya_number": 11},
})

# 3) Az-Zukhruf 43:77
q3 = next(q for q in quoted_spans if q["span_text"].startswith("وَنَادَوْا يَا مَالِكُ"))
results.append({
    "span_start": q3["span_start"],
    "span_end": q3["span_end"],
    "span_text": q3["span_text"],
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "الزخرف", "sura_number": 43, "aya_number": 77},
})

# 4) Abasa 80:15 'بِأَيْدِي سَفَرَةٍ'
results.append({
    "span_start": start1,
    "span_end": end1,
    "span_text": part1,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "عبس", "sura_number": 80, "aya_number": 15},
})

# 5) Abasa 80:16 'كِرَامٍ بَرَرَةٍ'
results.append({
    "span_start": start2,
    "span_end": end2,
    "span_text": part2,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "عبس", "sura_number": 80, "aya_number": 16},
})

# 6) Bukhari hadith quote between double parentheses
h1 = paren_spans[0]
results.append({
    "span_start": h1["span_start"],
    "span_end": h1["span_end"],
    "span_text": h1["span_text"],
    "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
    "hadeeth_reference": {"collection_name": "Sahih Bukhari", "book_number": 65, "hadith_number": 458},
})

# 7) Muslim hadith phrase
phrase = "ألا استحيي من رجل تستحيي منه الملائكة"
idx = main_text.find(phrase)
assert idx != -1
results.append({
    "span_start": idx,
    "span_end": idx + len(phrase),
    "span_text": phrase,
    "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
    "hadeeth_reference": {"collection_name": "Sahih Muslim", "book_number": 44, "hadith_number": 39},
})

# Build final JSON-like structure adding validity flags and corrected_text placeholders
final_list = []
for r in results:
    entry = {
        "span_start": r["span_start"],
        "span_end": r["span_end"],
        "span_text": r["span_text"],
        "citation_type": r["citation_type"],
        "is_valid": True,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": r["span_text"],
        "completely_invalid": False,
    }
    if r["citation_type"] == "Aya":
        entry["aya_reference"] = r["aya_reference"]
    else:
        entry["hadeeth_reference"] = r["hadeeth_reference"]
    final_list.append(entry)

len(final_list), final_list

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
